research.title: Proto-Object Visual Saliency Modeling
research.body: The nervous system can rapidly select important information from a visual scene and pay attention to it. Bottom-up saliency models use low-level features such as intensity, color, and orientation to generate a saliency map that predicts human fixations.

research.title: Hippocampal SLAM and Navigation
research.body: Creating biologically-plausible Simultaneous Localization And Mapping (SLAM) and navigation algorithms based on rodent hippocampus and entorhinal cortex.

research.title: Microbead Electrode
research.body: A micro-scale implantable bead that is small enough (<100 µm in diameter) so that multiple devices, potentially swarms of them, can be “injected” into the brain at different locations for neural recording and stimulation.

research.title: Wearable Sensors for Motor Activity in Neurological Patients
Integrate-and-Fire Array Transceiver
research.body: A micro-scale implantable bead that is small enough (<100 µm in diameter) so that multiple devices, potentially swarms of them, can be “injected” into the brain at different locations for neural recording and stimulation.

research.title: Active Ultrasound Pattern Injection System
research.body: An ultrasound tool tracking system with active echo elements, mid-plane detection, time/frequency modulation and arbitrary pattern injection functions.

research.title: Neuro-Modulated Diabetes Control (NeuMeDiC)
research.body: Investigating new bioelectronic approaches to pancreatic stimulation for diabetes control.

research.title: Spatio-temporal Compressive sensing Active Pixel Sensor (SCAPS)
research.body: Implementing temporal compressed sensing with pixel-wise coded exposure in an all-CMOS chip for high-framerate, low-power imaging.

research.title: Closed-loop Compressed Sensing System for Neural Recording
research.body: Using compressed-sensing techniques to condense entire neural signal waveforms with efficient on-chip circuits.
